jokes.text: Yowzer! The smelloscope is locked on to a collapsing magnetar.
jokes.setup: Scientific equipment for detecting cosmic phenomena
jokes.punchline: It's a 'smelloscope' that detects smells from space
jokes.explanation: Clever sci-fi absurdist humor that sets up the premise. The portmanteau 'smelloscope' is economical and character-appropriate for the Professor's inventions.

jokes.text: Oh, I never thought I'd live to smell the day.
jokes.setup: Common expression 'live to see the day'
jokes.punchline: Modified to 'smell the day' given the smelloscope context
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that builds on the smell theme. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Wait, how do you spell 'lavender'? / Only two Ls in 'smell.'
jokes.setup: Amy taking scientific dictation
jokes.punchline: She can't spell basic words but the Professor corrects the wrong word
jokes.explanation: Character-based humor about Amy's stereotypical ditziness. The Professor's misdirected correction adds a layer.

jokes.text: Woop-woop-woop-woop-woop... / Ugh, what are you rolling in now? Your own filth? / No, everyone's!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg making his signature sound and appearing filthy
jokes.punchline: He's not just rolling in his own filth but everyone's
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg character moment with good escalation structure. His response subverts expectations by being even worse than assumed.

jokes.text: I'll have to clear my nose with a nasal laser, or 'Naser'. / I've never heard of a Naser. / That's because it doesn't exist. So one of us will have to invent it.
jokes.setup: Professor needs to clear his nose
jokes.punchline: He makes up a portmanteau word for a device that doesn't exist
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with the Professor's tendency to invent things on the spot. The casual admission that it doesn't exist yet adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Hang on! I'm caught in my arm hole.
jokes.setup: Fry being called over for a test
jokes.punchline: He's stuck in his own clothing in an impossible way
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry incompetence moment. Simple but effective physical comedy that fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: In the market for crystals, eh? Well, sir, you're lookin' at one. Folks call me the Borax Kid.
jokes.setup: Someone offering to sell crystals
jokes.punchline: He calls himself a crystal and has the absurd nickname 'Borax Kid'
jokes.explanation: Good character introduction with Western parody elements. The self-identification as a crystal is charmingly absurd.

jokes.text: Pfft! Borax is just for laundry. / Not so. Why, it's also a leading cause of accidental poisoning.
jokes.setup: Dismissing borax as just a cleaning product
jokes.punchline: It's also great at accidentally poisoning people
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good misdirection. The Borax Kid's proud delivery of this concerning fact adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: Whoa! Words can have numbers in them?
jokes.setup: Professor mentions 'aluminum oxide' chemical name
jokes.punchline: Borax Kid is amazed that words can contain numbers
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-based humor that shows the Borax Kid's complete lack of scientific knowledge in an absurd way. The innocent wonder in his voice makes it funnier.

jokes.text: What birthstone is it? / Virgo. / Uh... can you describe it in a way that's less scientific?
jokes.setup: Borax Kid asking for less scientific description
jokes.punchline: He thinks astrology (Virgo) is more scientific than chemistry
jokes.explanation: Great irony where the character's understanding of 'scientific' is completely backwards. Shows his character perfectly.

jokes.text: My truth is I hate you!
jokes.setup: Crystal seller talking about speaking truth with blue crystals
jokes.punchline: Professor's truth is simply that he hates the guy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor moment - blunt, scientific, and dismissive. The economy of the line and its unexpected honesty make it very quotable.

jokes.text: She's an astrologer, just like you. / I'm an astronomer, you drunken idiot!
jokes.setup: Bender comparing astrologer to Professor
jokes.punchline: Professor's outraged correction about astronomy vs astrology
jokes.explanation: Classic confusion between similar-sounding scientific terms. The Professor's indignation and insult add character flavor.

jokes.text: We just met a cosmologist! / She sells holistic makeup with essential oils. / That's a cosmetologist!
jokes.setup: Continuing the confusion of scientific terms
jokes.punchline: Another mix-up between cosmologist and cosmetologist
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag of scientific term confusion. Less impactful as a repeat but maintains the pattern.

jokes.text: And what was she thinking? You're clearly an autumn. / Yeah, a little borax will set you right.
jokes.setup: Professor getting into beauty advice
jokes.punchline: Borax Kid suggests his poisonous product as a beauty treatment
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the borax character while showing the Professor getting caught up in pseudoscience despite himself.

jokes.text: Yet there is another path to knowledge. Faith. / Many people believe in a heaven, yet you do not call them crazy. / Yes, I do. To their faces. And behind their backs, I'm even ruder.
jokes.setup: Kif defending faith and suggesting Professor doesn't call believers crazy
jokes.punchline: Professor admits he does call them crazy, and he's even worse behind their backs
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment for the Professor. Great escalation structure and the admission of being even ruder behind people's backs is perfectly in character and very quotable.

jokes.text: You hurt my feeling. / I know Heaven is real because my grandma's there! / You're thinking of Mexico.
jokes.setup: Fry defending heaven because his grandma is there
jokes.punchline: Leela points out he's confusing heaven with Mexico
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection joke that plays on Fry's confusion. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier, and it's very much in character for both Fry and Leela.

jokes.text: Oh, right. I always mix those up 'cause they both have margaritas.
jokes.setup: Fry accepting he confused heaven and Mexico
jokes.punchline: His logic is they both have margaritas
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - absurd but internally consistent to him. The idea that heaven and Mexico are similar because of margaritas is wonderfully stupid.

jokes.text: I still don't believe it.
jokes.setup: People are literally floating up to heaven in front of him
jokes.punchline: Professor's stubborn denial of obvious evidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency joke. The Professor's commitment to skepticism even in the face of undeniable evidence is both admirable and absurd.

jokes.text: Why was I left behind?!
jokes.setup: The rapture is happening
jokes.punchline: Evil alien news anchor Morbo is confused why he wasn't taken to heaven
jokes.explanation: Great character-based irony. Morbo, who regularly threatens to destroy humanity, being confused about not making it to heaven is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I need to sneak out of work early to be with my family. / I'll let it slide this time, but if I do it again, I'm fired.
jokes.setup: Hermes asking to leave work early for the apocalypse
jokes.punchline: He threatens to fire himself if he does it again
jokes.explanation: Excellent Hermes bureaucracy joke. His obsession with proper workplace procedures even when talking to himself about the apocalypse is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Do you think I'll be left behind? What if they don't take pets? / Ow! I told you, you're not a pet! Now fetch me my slipper.
jokes.setup: Nibbler worried about being left behind as a pet
jokes.punchline: Leela insists he's not a pet while immediately treating him like one
jokes.explanation: Good irony joke with perfect timing. Leela's contradiction of herself within seconds is classic sitcom structure.

jokes.text: All the buttons are missing. / Strange... Who would eat nice, crunchy buttons? / Uh, just tie them with various lengths of wire.
jokes.setup: Lab coats are missing buttons
jokes.punchline: Professor wonders who would eat them while Bender (implied) is the obvious culprit
jokes.explanation: Standard Bender kleptomania joke. The Professor's innocent wondering while the answer is obvious adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Science is truth! Now kneel in the direction of MIT.
jokes.setup: Professor promoting science over cult behavior
jokes.punchline: He immediately acts like a cult leader himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony - the Professor creates a cult of science while denouncing cults. The specific reference to MIT makes it even better. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Science is truth! Now kneel in the direction of MIT.
jokes.setup: Professor promoting science over religion
jokes.punchline: Immediately creates religious-like ritual for science worship
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire showing how science can become cult-like, with perfect Professor hypocrisy

jokes.text: Stop him, Hermes! Do your limbo-jump!
jokes.setup: Their son is floating away in the rapture
jokes.punchline: She wants Hermes to use limbo skills to somehow jump and catch him
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Hermes' limbo abilities. The absurdity of using limbo to catch someone floating upward is perfect.

jokes.text: I'm stuck here with you?! / That's the worst-case scenario! / I was wrong!
jokes.setup: Hermes lamenting being stuck with his rival Barbados Slim
jokes.punchline: Barbados agrees it's the worst case, making Hermes realize it could be worse
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics with the rival relationship. The quick reversal when Hermes realizes even Barbados doesn't want to be stuck with him is well-timed.

jokes.text: Out holy book predicted this when your Dead Sea Scrolls were barely even sick.
jokes.setup: Religious leaders arguing about who predicted the rapture first
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'Dead Sea' with 'barely even sick' as if it's a progression of illness
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that treats 'dead' as a medical condition. The competitive religious context makes it funnier.

jokes.text: We worship data! Not a false cre-a-ta!
jokes.setup: Professor trying to create science chants
jokes.punchline: Forces 'creator' to rhyme with 'data' awkwardly
jokes.explanation: The forced rhyme and awkward pronunciation shows the Professor's attempt to make science cultish is failing. Character-appropriate awkwardness.

jokes.text: It's a cultured yogurt drink we grew in a beaker. With bacteria!
jokes.setup: Explaining their 'Cult-Aid' drink
jokes.punchline: It's literally cultured (bacterial) and made in lab equipment
jokes.explanation: Good science pun with 'cultured' working on multiple levels. The enthusiasm about bacteria is appropriately scientific and weird.

jokes.text: Almighty science is so powerful, it's only occasionally wrong!
jokes.setup: Professor promoting the power of science
jokes.punchline: His selling point is that science is only occasionally wrong
jokes.explanation: Great ironic sales pitch that accidentally undermines science. The Professor's enthusiasm for this weak selling point is perfect.

jokes.text: We don't need the megaphone. / It's for me. I can barely hear myself.
jokes.setup: Amy questioning the megaphone use
jokes.punchline: Professor admits it's for his own hearing, not projection
jokes.explanation: Good character moment about the Professor's age and hearing. Simple but effective physical comedy setup.

jokes.text: But you can use that to explain anything! / That's what's so great about it.
jokes.setup: Professor pointing out the flaw in 'God's plan' logic
jokes.punchline: Preacher treats this obvious flaw as a feature, not a bug
jokes.explanation: Excellent philosophical humor. The preacher's cheerful admission that religion's unfalsifiability is its strength is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: No way I'm ascendin' to Heaven with Barbados and LaBarbara. I'd rather go to Hell! / I'm here for you, friend. / I think I'm already there.
jokes.setup: Hermes preferring hell to being with his rivals
jokes.punchline: Bender offers support, making Hermes realize he's already in hell
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with dark humor. Hermes' realization that Bender's friendship might be worse than his other problems is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Man, that looked funny, but it really hurt. I think my skull's fractured.
jokes.setup: Fry falling after being tied to Leela
jokes.punchline: He acknowledges the visual comedy but emphasizes the real pain
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on slapstick comedy while still being slapstick. Fry's matter-of-fact delivery of serious injury is darkly funny.

jokes.text: Dammit! Which one of you idiots left the roof open? / Oh, right. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Professor angry about people escaping through roof
jokes.punchline: Bender casually admits it was him
jokes.explanation: Callback to repeated roof opening with typical Bender casual admission

jokes.text: Leela's gone. And all I have to remember her by is this boot.
jokes.setup: Fry mourning Leela's departure to heaven
jokes.punchline: He's holding her boot, implying it came off when she floated away
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that plays on cartoon physics. The boot coming off during rapture ascension is absurdly practical.

jokes.text: I know Leela and I will be reunited someday, either in Heaven, or one big coffin.
jokes.setup: Fry being romantic about reunion with Leela
jokes.punchline: His backup plan is shared death/burial
jokes.explanation: Dark romantic humor that's very Fry. The matter-of-fact delivery of this morbid backup plan makes it funnier.

jokes.text: A fusion canon? Too powerful.
jokes.setup: Professor testing what could make people float
jokes.punchline: A weapon of mass destruction is just too powerful for the job
jokes.explanation: The casual consideration of using a fusion cannon for people-floating is appropriately Professor-like in its extreme overkill.

jokes.text: Get her! But be gentle.
jokes.setup: Kif ordering Amy's kidnapping
jokes.punchline: He wants them to be gentle while kidnapping her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kif character moment - trying to be considerate even while doing something terrible. The contradiction is very him.

jokes.text: Then quit sittin' on your premium-sized ass, Hermes.
jokes.setup: Bender motivating Hermes to do evil deeds
jokes.punchline: He calls Hermes' butt 'premium-sized' as an insult/description
jokes.explanation: Bender's creative way of calling someone fat that sounds almost complimentary. The 'premium' descriptor is amusingly corporate.

jokes.text: Oopsie-dinkle. I meant to close that.
jokes.setup: Hermes trying to do evil deeds
jokes.punchline: His version of evil is saying 'oopsie-dinkle' while causing mayhem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes character moment. His bureaucratic politeness makes his attempts at evil adorably ineffective. 'Oopsie-dinkle' is wonderfully quotable.

jokes.text: You know my parents don't pay ransom.
jokes.setup: Amy being kidnapped
jokes.punchline: She immediately points out her parents won't pay, ruining the kidnappers' plan
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Amy knows her parents don't care that much. Her practical response to kidnapping is very her.

jokes.text: I hope the pillowcase wasn't too scratchy. I got the highest thread count I could.
jokes.setup: Kif apologizing for kidnapping Amy
jokes.punchline: He's worried about the comfort of the kidnapping hood
jokes.explanation: Excellent Kif character moment - considerate even while committing crimes. The thread count detail makes it even more absurdly thoughtful.

jokes.text: Um, will you be using actual words at some point? / Nope, just spirit sounds.
jokes.setup: Grand Midwife making nonsense spiritual sounds
jokes.punchline: She admits she won't be using real words, just 'spirit sounds'
jokes.explanation: Good parody of new-age spirituality. The matter-of-fact admission that it's just nonsense sounds is perfect.

jokes.text: It wasn't just yogurt, it was a yogurt beverage!
jokes.setup: Amy downplaying the cult's yogurt drink
jokes.punchline: Kif's dramatic correction that it was a beverage, not just yogurt
jokes.explanation: Kif's dramatic emphasis on the distinction between yogurt and yogurt beverage shows his overreaction to the 'cult.'

jokes.text: Sorry, it takes five more hours.
jokes.setup: Amy and Kif trying to leave the spiritual intervention
jokes.punchline: The fake ceremony has a ridiculously long mandatory duration
jokes.explanation: Parody of how spiritual/therapeutic sessions can drag on unnecessarily. The arbitrary time requirement is absurd.

jokes.text: This here's a robbery. Gimme all your knives!
jokes.setup: Hermes attempting to rob a store
jokes.punchline: He demands knives instead of money, which makes no sense for a robbery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes character moment - even his robbery is bureaucratically confused. Demanding knives instead of money shows he doesn't understand crime.

jokes.text: Fry's dead. / Oh, what have I done?! Now I'm down to just two pallbearers.
jokes.setup: Professor lamenting Fry's apparent death
jokes.punchline: Bender only cares about having enough pallbearers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender selfishness turning death into personal inconvenience

jokes.text: Now I'm down to just two pallbearers.
jokes.setup: Fry appearing to be dead
jokes.punchline: Professor's practical concern about his funeral arrangements
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark Professor humor showing his self-centered priorities even when mourning. Very characteristic.

jokes.text: I'm gonna try fist-to-crotch resuscitation.
jokes.setup: Fry appears dead and needs resuscitation
jokes.punchline: Bender's version of CPR involves punching Fry in the crotch
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender moment - his solution to everything is violence. The medical-sounding term makes the crotch punch seem legitimate.

jokes.text: Oh, Heavenly Father, let me first apologize for referring to you as 'Magic Space Grandpa' for over a century.
jokes.setup: Professor finally turning to prayer
jokes.punchline: He admits to calling God 'Magic Space Grandpa' for a century
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment that's both irreverent and sincere. 'Magic Space Grandpa' is hilariously dismissive yet oddly endearing. Very quotable.

jokes.text: no onions
jokes.setup: Professor praying for food for the hungry
jokes.punchline: He adds 'no onions' to his prayer like it's a food order
jokes.explanation: Perfect detail that shows the Professor treating prayer like placing an order. The casual insertion into a desperate prayer is very funny.

jokes.text: The Almighty has blessed us with snack food. And not just any snack food, the most irresistible snack food ever made!
jokes.setup: Professor attributing cheese puff to divine intervention
jokes.punchline: Escalates to calling it the most irresistible snack food ever
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Professor's temporary religious conversion over junk food

jokes.text: I don't remember leaving the roof open for a third time.
jokes.setup: Another roof opening incident
jokes.punchline: Professor's admission this keeps happening suggests a pattern of forgetfulness
jokes.explanation: Running gag about the Professor's forgetfulness. The specific count of three makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Huh. I hope his legs aren't in there.
jokes.setup: Fry being pulled through the roof by mysterious force
jokes.punchline: Professor's casual concern about dismemberment
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor delivered with typical Professor casualness. The understated delivery sells the macabre concern.

jokes.text: Huh. I hope his legs aren't in there.
jokes.setup: Roof closes on escaping person
jokes.punchline: Casual concern about potential dismemberment
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Professor's casual delivery about potential gore

jokes.text: It wasn't some supernatural power. It was a simple hook! A simple trans-phase plasma hook on a simple mono-molecular force-filament!
jokes.setup: Professor discovering the scientific explanation
jokes.punchline: His 'simple' explanation gets increasingly complex and technical
jokes.explanation: Great escalation where 'simple' becomes incredibly complex. Perfect Professor character moment about his perspective on complexity.

jokes.text: What is that thing, Chay? / We are witnessin' somethin' weird, bro. / It's a baby whale!
jokes.setup: Space fishermen examining their catch
jokes.punchline: They think the human ship is a baby whale
jokes.explanation: Good perspective flip joke. The fishermen's accent and confusion add character flavor to the role reversal.

jokes.text: Nah, it's not a whale, but it's wicked ugly.
jokes.setup: Examining the human spaceship
jokes.punchline: Calls the Planet Express ship 'wicked ugly'
jokes.explanation: Simple insult comedy with Boston accent flavor. The casual dismissal of the ship's appearance is appropriately fisherman-like.

jokes.text: Eh, I've been called worse.
jokes.setup: Being called 'bycatch' by the fishermen
jokes.punchline: Leela's casual acceptance of the insult
jokes.explanation: Standard Leela response to insults. Her thick skin about appearance jokes is well-established.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, you're wicked smaht. 'Duh, I'm eatin' cheese puffs on a hook 'cause I'm a brainiac!'
jokes.setup: Humans claiming to be intelligent
jokes.punchline: Fisherman mocks their intelligence while pointing out they fell for bait
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comeback that has a valid point. The accent and mockery delivery make it effective.

jokes.text: How dare you space-townies drop that sick burn!
jokes.setup: Space fisherman insulting the humans
jokes.punchline: Bender admits it was a good insult while being offended
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor where Bender acknowledges good comedy writing. His outrage at being accurately burned is perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: 'God'? / The Coast Gahd!
jokes.setup: Professor hearing about 'God' arrival
jokes.punchline: It's the Coast Guard with Boston accent pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection based on accent. Sets up the bureaucratic resolution while playing on religious expectations.

jokes.text: First off, you failed to submit the $18 fee to renew your license.
jokes.setup: Major illegal fishing operation being investigated
jokes.punchline: The first violation is an $18 licensing fee
jokes.explanation: Perfect bureaucratic comedy. The tiny fee amount for such a massive operation is absurdly anticlimactic.

jokes.text: I got the Dunkins. / You were supposed to get the license!
jokes.setup: Partners blaming each other for licensing failure
jokes.punchline: One got donuts instead of handling paperwork
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing typical partner blame dynamics. The Dunkin' Donuts reference adds Boston flavor.

jokes.text: I have no choice but to issue the first of three courtesy warnings.
jokes.setup: Major crimes requiring punishment
jokes.punchline: The severe penalty is just the first of three warnings
jokes.explanation: Excellent bureaucratic anticlimax. The formal language makes the completely inadequate response even funnier.

jokes.text: You can keep that one.
jokes.setup: Coast Guard ordering release of all catches
jokes.punchline: Making exception for one particularly undesirable human
jokes.explanation: Standard visual gag about one character being particularly undesirable. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You have to wonder, could the whole concept of ascending to Heaven have come from ancient people being caught by space fishermen? / Maybe. Or, it could be a miracle. It's impossible to say.
jokes.setup: Amy suggesting rational explanation for religious concepts
jokes.punchline: Professor admits both explanations are equally possible
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on the episode's theme. Shows character growth for the Professor in admitting uncertainty.

jokes.text: Is it possible all this has broadened your mind a little? / No.
jokes.setup: Amy suggesting the Professor has grown from the experience
jokes.punchline: His immediate, flat 'No' contradicts his previous open-minded statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending that undercuts any character growth. The timing and economy of 'No' is perfection. Classic Professor stubbornness that's highly quotable.
